persons.arguments_against: Two distinct Logans share this surname. Sir William Marston Logan (W.M., b.1889) is an ADMINISTRATOR: the entry gives senior assistant secretary Kenya from 1924 (O.B.E.) and chief secretary Northern Rhodesia 1937 (C.M.G.) — matched by the two W. M. Logan stints assigned here. The three 'W. E. M. Logan' stints (Kenya/Tanganyika/Uganda, 1949-62) are a FORESTER (Conservator of Forests) with a different middle initial and an entirely different career — a different person, left unassigned.
persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person serving the same colony in the same years and offices, or a printed birth/death date excluding these years.

unassigned_stints.reason: W. E. M. Logan, Assistant Conservator of Forests — a forester, different middle initial; not this administrator.
W. E. M. Logan, Deputy Chief Conservator of Forests — the forester, not this man.
W. E. M. Logan, Chief Conservator of Forests — the forester, not this man.

notes: production high-value slice batch 3, hand-adjudicated (Claude Code, in-session)
